steps.title: Use Desmos to fit the quadratic table
steps.content: A quadratic is determined by 3 points, so the fastest method is to enter the table in Desmos and run a quadratic regression. Use $x_1=[-1,0,1]$, $y_1=[10,14,20]$, then type $y_1 \sim a x_1^2+b x_1+c$. Desmos returns $a=1$, $b=5$, and $c=14$, so the model is $y=x^2+5x+14$.

steps.title: Match the model to the choices
steps.content: The equation $f(x)=x^2+5x+14$ is choice D. A quick substitution confirms it: $f(-1)=1-5+14=10$, $f(0)=14$, and $f(1)=1+5+14=20$. All three table rows match, so choice D — D is correct.
